etymology.description: semantic 金(金) (jīn, "metal") + phonetic 羅(羅) (luó) -> metal instrument -> gong
etymology.interpretation: 鑼 (luó, "gong") is a large metal instrument that produces a resonant sound. The character uses 金(金) (jīn, "metal") as the semantic component, indicating it is made of metal. The phonetic component 羅(羅) (luó) suggests its pronunciation.
etymology.simplification: 鑼 was simplified to 锣 by removing the complex component 羅(羅) and replacing it with simplified form that suggests sound.

examples.chinese: 当钟楼敲响时(當鐘樓敲響時)，村里的人们都停下手中的活儿(村裡的人們都停下手中的活兒)。
examples.pinyin: Dāng zhōng lóu qiāo xiǎng shí, cūn lǐ de rénmen dōu tíng xià shǒuzhōng de huó'er.
examples.english: When the bell tower rings, the villagers all stop their work.

examples.chinese: 他用力敲打鑼(他用力敲打鑼)，发出巨大的声音(發出巨大的聲音)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yòng lì qiāo dǎ luó, fā chū jùdà de shēngyīn.
examples.english: He strikes the gong forcefully, producing a loud sound.

examples.chinese: 古老的仪式中(古老的儀式中)，總是可以听到鑼聲(總是可以聽到鑼聲)。
examples.pinyin: Gǔlǎo de yíshì zhōng, zǒng shì kěyǐ tīng dào luó shēng.
examples.english: In ancient ceremonies, the sound of the gong can always be heard.
